Who I Am

About Me

My name is Joseph Chiu and I'm a senior at Mission San Jose High School in Fremont, California. I got into sports photography because I wanted a reason to stay close to the games I grew up watching, and somewhere along the way it turned into something I actually care a lot about.

I shoot mostly sports: basketball, water polo, and live events like Dance Bazzi. What I like about it is that you can't really plan the shot. You just have to know the game well enough to be in the right spot and hope your timing is good. I've missed plenty of shots, but the ones that work out make it worth it.

Outside of class I play pickup basketball pretty regularly, which honestly helps when I'm shooting games because I already know where the action is going. I'm also a big music fan, especially Bazzi, which is part of why covering Dance Bazzi was so fun for me. This portfolio is a collection of my best work from this school year across three different projects.

My Work

Portfolio

Basketball is where I started taking photography seriously. I shot these games courtside at Mission San Jose, trying to get as close to the action as possible without getting in the way. Gym lighting is tricky because it's usually pretty harsh and uneven, so a lot of this was figuring out the right shutter speed to freeze movement without losing too much light. I focused on the moments right before and after big plays, layups, contested shots, and the bench reactions. Playing basketball myself made it easier to anticipate where things were headed, which helped a lot with timing.

The Closeout
A defender flies in to contest the ball handler driving baseline. Shot low from the corner to get the gym and the basket in the frame.
Fist Pump
A Mission San Jose player celebrates after scoring. The wide angle and low position made the whole court the backdrop.
Pull Up
Number 32 rises up for a mid-range jumper with defenders closing in. Caught at the peak of his release with the Mission San Jose gym wall visible behind him.

Dance Bazzi is one of the biggest events at Mission San Jose and I was glad I got to shoot it this year. It's a different kind of challenge compared to sports because the lighting is always changing and you're trying to capture a specific moment in a performance rather than just reacting to whatever happens. I spent a lot of time figuring out angles from the audience and the sides of the stage. Stage lighting can wash things out or create really cool shadows depending on where you are, so positioning mattered a lot. These photos ended up being some of my favorites from the whole year just because of how dynamic everything looked.

Hair Whip
A performer mid-turn with her hair sweeping across the frame. The blue stage lighting and dark background made the sparkle in the costume pop.
Center Stage
Three dancers in matching red and white skirts spin in sync. Captured the lead performer at the front with her arms fully extended toward the crowd.
The Dip
A duet captured at the lowest point of a dip. Shot against the dark curtain backdrop which helped isolate the two performers and keep the focus on them.
In the Crowd
A solo performer steps forward from the full cast behind her. The energy from the group in the background made this one of the best crowd shots from the night.

Water polo was honestly one of the harder things I've shot this year. The pool lighting is tough, the water reflects everything, and the athletes are moving fast in a confined space. I shot from pool deck level to stay as close to the water as I could, which gave the photos a better angle than shooting from the stands. A lot of it came down to timing because the best moments happen when someone is coming up out of the water or fighting for position. I had to trash a lot of shots before I started getting ones I was happy with, but the process of figuring it out was worth it.

Point Blank
A player rises out of the water right at the goal post for a close-range shot. The frozen water droplets around her and the packed crowd in the background came together perfectly.
Contested Pass
Two players battle for possession mid-pool with the goal frame visible in the distance. Shot at night which gave the pool water a deep teal color that I really liked.
Sideline Talk
The coach crouches at the pool edge giving instructions to players huddled at the wall during a break. Shot from water level, it captures the strategy side of the sport people don't usually see.
